note: `match` is a RegExpMatchArray produced by `Str.matchAll` + `A.fromIterable`; `match[3]` reads a regex capture group (the closing-fence group) and is compared to `undefined` to detect an unterminated fence. Converting regex-group index access to `A.get` (returning an Option) changes the ergonomics of capture-group inspection; a human should confirm whether `A.get(match, 3)` + Option handling is desirable here or whether this index access should be left as-is.

`match` is a RegExpMatchArray (regex capture groups). `match[1]` reads the fence metadata capture group with a `?? ""` fallback. This is regex capture-group access rather than ordinary array element access; converting to `A.get(match, 1)` + `O.getOrElse` is possible but semantically awkward for capture groups, so flag for human decision.

`match` is a RegExpMatchArray (regex capture groups). `match[2]` reads the fenced code-body capture group with a `?? ""` fallback. Same regex capture-group consideration as the other `match[...]` sites; flag for human decision rather than mechanically rewriting to `A.get`.

`tokens` is a `ReadonlyArray<string>` produced by `Str.split(/\s+/)`. `tokens[0] ?? ""` reads the first element with an empty-string fallback, i.e. `pipe(tokens, A.head, O.getOrElse(thunkEmptyStr))`. `A` is already imported in this module.
